Title: 'No problem' fielding non-Malay candidate in Permas, says Mat Sabu
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Amanah president Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu has defended the party's decision to field Sharon Teo Siew Hui in Permas for the Johor election despite a boycott by the party's Pasir Gudang division.
He said the candidate selection had been finalised and there was no issue with the decision.
"There is no problem. We have settled everything. God willing," he told reporters after the official send-off ceremony for the export of fresh MD2 pineapples to New Zealand at the Farmers' Organisation Authority (LPP) headquarters here today.
He was responding to questions on the decision by Amanah Pasir Gudang to boycott Teo's candidacy.
Earlier, Amanah Pasir Gudang announced it would
boycott the Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate for Permas
, who was named by PH chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim on June 22.
Its assistant secretary Haini Harun said the decision was made unanimously as the division continued to support Pasir Gudang Amanah chief Syed Othman Abdullah, whom it considered a more suitable candidate based on his leadership and grassroots work in the constituency.
He said the division had repeatedly raised objections to the possibility of an outsider being fielded but had not received any response.
According to Haini, the selection of a candidate unfamiliar to grassroots machinery would make it more challenging to mobilise campaign efforts within a short period.
"As such, we have decided to boycott the candidate and will not assist in her campaign.
"However, we will not obstruct or interfere with campaign machinery brought in from outside the constituency," he said.
Amanah Johor had earlier announced Teo as its candidate for Permas, making her the party's only non-Malay and non-Muslim candidate in the Johor election.
The former Johor Amanah assistant secretary and Johor Warda chief said her candidacy reflected Amanah's commitment to being a multiracial party.
On June 22, PH announced its 56 candidates for the Johor election, with Amanah fielding 19 candidates, including 13 new faces.
Nomination day is set for June 27, followed by early voting on July 7 and polling on July 11.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MARKET PULSE PM JUNE 24, 2026 [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The FBM KLCI closed higher today as bargain hunting activities emerged following the recent market weakness.
Gains were led by healthcare stocks, followed by telecommunication and banking counters, as investors rotated into sectors that had experienced recent price corrections.

Following the trend, Ethereum traded higher at RM6,934, while Solana stood at RM288.
That wraps up today's Market Pulse.

Title: Indonesia offers US$121 bil investment opportunities for developing EV battery ecosystem
Article:
JAKARTA: Indonesia is offering investment opportunities worth US$121 billion (US$1 = RM4.14) to develop its national electric vehicle (EV) battery manufacturing ecosystem, as the country seeks to strengthen its position in the global battery supply chain.
Indonesia's Ministry of Investment and Downstreaming Director of Downstreaming Strategy and Governance Ahmad Faisal Suralaga said the opportunities cover the entire EV battery value chain, from mineral processing and battery materials to battery and electric vehicle manufacturing.
Speaking at the Korea-Indonesia Economic Partnership Forum in Jakarta on Wednesday, he said Indonesia possesses four of the six key raw materials required for EV battery production, namely nickel, bauxite, manganese and copper, providing a strong foundation for the development of an integrated battery industry.
"By 2045, Indonesia intends to become one of the top five EV battery producers in the world. This is possible because downstreaming creates the highest added value.
"For example, the value of nickel can increase by as much as 67 times if it is processed into EV batteries," he said according to Antara News Agency.
Ahmad said Indonesia is the world's largest nickel producer, accounting for 42 per cent of global nickel resources, while its downstreaming strategy has also been expanded to 28 other commodities with competitive advantages.
Investment opportunities are focused on advanced smelters, EV battery cell manufacturing and other segments of the supply chain, he said, noting that Indonesia is among the few countries with an integrated industry spanning the entire EV battery ecosystem.
Ahmad added that through investment and downstreaming, Indonesia estimates total investment potential could reach US$618 billion, generate about US$857 billion in exports and create more than three million jobs.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MMEA detains three foreign workers at fish cage off Teluk Bahang
Article:
PENANG: The Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) detained three foreign workers at a fish cage off Teluk Bahang here yesterday.
Penang maritime director Captain Muhammad Suffi Mohd Ramli said the operation was carried out about 1pm following intelligence received by the state maritime operations centre.
He said a patrol boat was deployed to inspect a fish cage located about 0.1 nautical mile north of Teluk Bahang waters.
"Checks revealed that three foreign workers failed to produce valid work permits. They comprised two Myanmar nationals and one Bangladeshi.
"All three men were detained and handed over to the investigating officer at the Penang Maritime headquarters for further action," he said in a statement today.
Suffi said the trio, aged between 21 and 39, are suspected of committing offences under Section 6(1)(c) of the Immigration Act 1959/63 for being in Malaysia without valid documents or permits, and Section 5(2) of the same Act for entering or leaving the country without passing through an authorised entry point.
He said the fish cage operator is also suspected of committing an offence under Section 13(a) of the Fisheries Act 1985 for operating an aquaculture system without a valid licence.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ringgit extends gains on strong Malaysian economic data
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit extended its gains to close higher against the US dollar and other major currencies, as well as ASEAN peers, on Wednesday, supported by stronger-than-expected economic data in Malaysia, which boosted investor confidence and market sentiment.
At 6 pm, the local note appreciated to 4.1355/1400 against the greenback from 4.1380/1430 at Tuesday's close.
According to the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM), Malaysia's foreign direct investment (FDI) rose 41.2 per cent to RM65.9 billion in 2025 from RM46.7 billion in 2024, driven mainly by equity injections from foreign investors and inflows into debt instruments.
DOSM noted that the country's cumulative FDI position grew to RM1.09 trillion at end-2025, accounting for 53.7 per cent of gross domestic product (GDP), compared with 51.4 per cent in 2024, reflecting sustained confidence among foreign investors in Malaysia's economy.
Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said the encouraging economic data continued to support the ringgit, reflecting growing confidence in Malaysia's economic outlook.
"The growth in FDI supports a positive view of Malaysia's economic fundamentals, as the country's balance of payments (BOP) indicates positive inflows into Malaysia. This means demand for the ringgit remains healthy," he told Bernama.
Mohd Afzanizam also said the statement by Bank Negara Malaysia's (BNM) Financial Markets Committee (FMC) today indicated that the recent movements in the ringgit and regional currencies were driven by global developments, such as expectations of a United States (US) interest rate increase.
"The FMC also acknowledged non-resident outflows, which were predominantly due to portfolio adjustments by foreign funds following the ringgit's strong performance in the early part of the year.
"On that note, the FMC statement suggests that BNM is cognisant of the current market dynamics and would respond accordingly to ensure an orderly currency market," he said.
Furthermore, he said fears over an interest rate hike in the US continue to influence market sentiment, as the latest US  Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) readings for June, which rose more than expected, suggest that business confidence remains fairly resilient.
"The higher-than-expected PMI readings make the case for a US interest rate hike increasingly credible, with the probability of a rate increase at the September Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting rising to 54.7 per cent," he added.
At the close, the ringgit was stronger against a basket of major currencies.
It gained against the British pound to 5.4481/4540 from 5.4750/4816 at Tuesday's close, rose versus the euro to 4.6921/6972 from 4.7223/7280 yesterday, and was firmer vis-à-vis the Japanese yen at 2.5567/5595 from 2.5638/5669 previously.
The local note was also higher against regional currencies.
It appreciated against the Singapore dollar to 3.1848/1885 from 3.1959/2000 at Tuesday's close, and increased versus the Thai baht to 12.3658/3845 from 12.4845/5053 previously.
The ringgit also advanced versus the Indonesian rupiah at  230.3/230.7 from 231.6/232.0 yesterday and strengthened against the Philippine peso to 6.72/6.73 from 6.74/6.75 previously.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: KL cable theft: Cops record major drop in cases
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Cable theft cases in the capital have declined significantly, with 71 reports recorded between January and May this year compared with 205 cases throughout 2025.
Kuala Lumpur police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said police remained committed to combating cable theft through continuous enforcement operations across the city.
He said 171 suspects were arrested in connection with cable theft cases last year involving telecommunications cables, railway infrastructure and other cable-related offences.
Police also opened 193 investigation papers, with 60 cases successfully prosecuted in court.
"For the first five months of this year, police arrested 85 suspects following 71 reported cases.
"The arrests comprised 50 suspects linked to telecommunications cable theft, 17 involving railway cable theft and 18 connected to other cable-related offences," he said in a statement today.
A total of 67 investigation papers were opened during the period, with 12 cases already resulting in court charges.
Fadil said the combined value of exhibits seized since last year amounted to RM629,000, involving 357 items.
He said cable theft was not merely a property crime but one that directly affected public safety, economic activity and the continuity of essential services.
"Such offences can disrupt telecommunications services, business operations and government service delivery, while also increasing infrastructure repair and maintenance costs.
"Cable theft also affects economic productivity and can result in financial losses. In some cases, illegally cutting or removing cables can damage existing infrastructure and create safety risks, including electrical shocks and fires," he said.
Investigations are conducted under Section 379 of the Penal Code and Section 235 of the Communications and Multimedia Act 1998.
Fadil urged the public to assist police by providing information on cable theft activities.
Information can be channelled through the Kuala Lumpur police hotline at 03-2115 9999 or at the nearest police station.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Armed Forces Cooperative declares 7.3 pct dividend, pays out RM126.2 mil
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Koperasi Angkatan Tentera Malaysia Bhd (Koperasi Tentera) has declared a 7.3 per cent dividend involving a total payout of RM126.2 million to more than 154,000 members for the financial year ended 2025.
Deputy Defence Minister Adly Zahari announced the dividend at the cooperative's 60th Annual General Meeting here today.
He said Koperasi Tentera recorded a net income of RM278 million for the financial year ended 2025, up from RM253 million in 2024.
"I am proud of Koperasi Tentera's achievement in maintaining its status as a premier cooperative with assets exceeding RM3 billion.
"More importantly, these achievements are built on six decades of trust and the continued commitment of members who have chosen to grow together with Koperasi Tentera," he said in his speech.
Adly described the cooperative as a strategic asset for the Malaysian Armed Forces (ATM) community, built on trust, solidarity and a shared responsibility to safeguard members' welfare.
"The success of a cooperative should not be measured solely by profit figures, but by the extent to which its achievements and benefits can be returned to its members," he said.
Meanwhile, Koperasi Tentera chairman Lt Gen (Rtd) Datuk Zaidan Atan said the increase in revenue was driven by growth in personal financing, small and medium enterprise financing, and Ar-Rahnu services, as well as higher income from insurance and investment activities.
He said the cooperative had 154,797 members as at December 2025, comprising 117,301 serving military personnel, 9,618 civil servants and 27,878 retired military personnel.
Zaidan said Koperasi Tentera, which is approaching its 66th anniversary, has retained its Premier Cooperative status in the Top 100 Cooperatives Profile compiled by the Malaysia Cooperative Societies Commission for the sixth consecutive year.
Also present were Chief of Defence Force and Koperasi Tentera president Gen Tan Sri Malek Razak Sulaiman, Army chief Gen Tan Sri Azhan Md Othman, Navy chief Admiral Tan Sri Dr Zulhelmy Ithnain and Air Force chief Gen Tan Sri Muhamad Norazlan Aris. The three service chiefs are also Koperasi Tentera vice-presidents.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Dr Adham Baba, ex-Bersatu rep among Johor BN candidates
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Former Endau assemblyman Alwiyah Talib and former health minister Datuk Seri Dr Adham Baba were among the 56 Johor Barisan Nasional candidates who will contest in the state election.
The announcement of their candidacy was made by Johor BN chief Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi during a ceremony here today.
Alwiyah won the seat in 2018 when she contested under Umno before defecting to Bersatu.
In the 2022 state election, Alwiyah retained the seat when she polled 8,433 votes to defeat four other candidates.
Onn Hafiz also announced that Dr Adham is the BN candidate for Pasir Raja.
Adham, who is also the Tenggara Umno division chief, previously served as the Pasir Raja assemblyman for two terms (2008–2018) and as the Tenggara member of Parliament for two terms (2004–2008 and 2018–2022).
Meanwhile, one of the state executive councillors has been dropped as a BN candidate for the polls.
Former chairman of the Women, Family and Community Development Committee, Khairin-Nisa Ismail @ Md On, who is also the former Serom state assemblyman, was not named as a candidate in this state election.
Onn Hafiz named a new face and young candidate, Nadhirah Afiqah Abdull Rahim, for the Serom seat.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: High Court bars Papagomo from posting defamatory content against MCMC
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The High Court has granted an ex-parte interim injunction barring blogger Wan Muhammad Azri Wan Deris, better known as Papagomo, from publishing defamatory statements against the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) or disseminating harmful content online.
In a statement today, MCMC said the injunction also restrains Wan Muhammad Azri from using any social media platform, whether through his existing accounts or newly created ones, to publish or spread such content.
The commission said the order will remain in force pending an inter partes hearing or until further directions are issued by the court.
It said the civil action was initiated following a
notice of claim
served on Wan Muhammad Azri on April 9 over allegations that the commission had blocked a news report published by news portal MalaysiaGazette.
The report concerned a court case involving Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil and Wan Muhammad Azri.
MCMC denied it had blocked the report, stressing that the allegation was untrue and that it was not involved, nor did it request any media organisation or platform to block the content.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pakistan court sentences Balochistan rights activist to life in jail
Article:
QUETTA: A Pakistani court has sentenced a prominent rights activist from southern Balochistan province to life imprisonment on murder and terror charges, drawing condemnation from civil liberties advocates this week.
Mahrang Baloch, 33, was arrested in 2025 on charges stemming from a protest in the coastal city of Gwadar the previous year, which sparked clashes that left a paramilitary soldier dead.
Baloch, the founder of the Baloch Yakjehti Committee (BYC), was handed the sentence in an anti-terrorism court in provincial capital Quetta on Monday, according to a copy of the decision.
Her lawyer, Israr Jattak, told AFP his client had boycotted the earlier court hearings "because we didn't have any trust in the court. This wasn't an ordinary case."
The court held that Baloch was an active participant in the "unlawful assembly" and "shared the common object of committing the murder" of the paramilitiary force member.
The prosecution had alleged that the security force member was killed after being hit by sticks and stones.
Baloch's legal team denied the charges, and her sister Nadia Baloch told AFP they would appeal the decision in a higher court.
The BYC has denounced extrajudicial killings, forced disappearances and arbitrary arrests in Balochistan – Pakistan's most resource-rich province, where around half the population live below the poverty line.
Authorities deny breaching civil liberties and say ramped-up security is necessary to address a deadly insurgency in the border province.
International rights group Amnesty International called for the immediate release of Mahrang Baloch and other activists detained in the case.
"This verdict, which is an affront to the right to a fair trial, demonstrates how Pakistan's anti-terrorism laws are being cynically misused to silence peaceful dissent," said Isabelle Lassee, Amnesty International's Acting Regional Director for South Asia, in a statement on Tuesday.
She added that Mahrang Baloch and BYC organisers "are being targeted solely for their human rights work."
Authorities have previously denied accusations of politicisation of Pakistan's legal system.
The Human Rights Commission of Pakistan said the government was pursuing "its policy of dealing with advocacy for fundamental rights in the same way as it deals with militancy."
Security forces are battling increasing separatist militant attacks in Balochistan, which borders Afghanistan and Iran.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Hilux driver escapes police chase by swerving into oncoming traffic
Article:
AMPANG JAYA: Police have launched a manhunt for a driver who led authorities on a high-speed chase before escaping by driving over a road divider and into oncoming traffic early Monday morning.
Ampang Jaya police chief Assistant Commissioner Khairul Anuar Khalid said the incident began around 3.16am when a Toyota Hilux driver fled a roadblock mounted by Cheras police at the Billion Roundabout.
A motorcycle officer from the Cheras police station's Crime Prevention Unit pursued the vehicle as it sped along the Middle Ring Road 2 towards Jalan Pandan Utama.
Two multi-purpose vehicle (MPV) patrol units from the Pandan Indah police station joined the pursuit after receiving a request for assistance from the officer.
"Two MPVs from the Pandan Indah station were involved in the chase alongside the motorcycle officer," he said.
However, the suspect managed to evade capture.
"The suspect suddenly drove over the road divider, entered the opposite lane and sped away before disappearing from sight," he said.
No arrests have been made and investigations are ongoing to identify the driver and locate the vehicle.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Keyfield bags RM229mil charter contracts
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Keyfield International Bhd has secured 13 charter contracts worth RM229 million across Malaysia, Thailand and the Middle East.
The offshore vessel provider said the contracts, which also include extension options worth RM41 million, further diversify Keyfield's international customer base and reinforce its position as one of Malaysia's leading offshore vessel owners and operators.
The addition of the new contract brings Keyfield's charter contract wins to 21 within the first half of 2026.
Group chief executive officer and executive director Datuk Darren Kee Chit Huei said the contracts involve a diverse mix of vessel categories within its fleet as well as third-party vessels.
The vessels will serve various petroleum arrangement contractors and oil and gas contractors across Malaysia, Thailand and the Middle East.
"The contracts will commence in the second and third quarter of 2026, with firm charter periods ranging from one month to 17 months.
"Some of the contracts also include extension options ranging from 15 days to five months," he said.
Currently 12 of its 14 vessels are on hire, with the other two projected to be on hire by early second half of 2026 upon completion of ongoing operational inspections by its customers, Kee added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Keiko Fujimori: Peru's biggest political loser poised to win election
Article:
PERU'S Keiko Fujimori is a perennial political loser. She has run for the nation's presidency three elections in a row – and three times, she has lost.
But on this, her fourth attempt, the daughter of disgraced late president Alberto Fujimori is on the verge of winning.
With almost all the ballots counted, Fujimori held 50.12 per cent of the vote, a margin of just over 43,000 over her leftist rival Roberto Sanchez, according to electoral authorities.
The 51-year-old came head-to-head with Sanchez in a June 7 runoff, under the still-divisive legacy of her father who ruled Peru in the 1990s.
Fujimori's surname is recognised across the Andean nation, which has had eight presidents since 2016.
"It is a brand that is well positioned, whether you like it or not," said political scientist Jorge Aragon.
This fourth attempt "may be the one that works," he said.
Poised and polished, with a sharp pantsuit and practiced smile, Fujimori looks like someone raised for politics.
A business administrator educated in the United States, she has served as a lawmaker and leads her party.
She became first lady at 19, after her mother publicly broke with Alberto Fujimori, and grew up alongside heads of state and foreign leaders.
Alberto Fujimori led Peru through the turbulent 1990s, crushing Maoist Shining Path rebels and taming hyperinflation.
But he was later disgraced, exiled and jailed for corruption and crimes against humanity.
For decades, the Fujimori name has helped and haunted Keiko, giving her instant recognition, loyal voters and deep political networks – but also plenty of critics.
"I miss him," she told AFP in April. "But everywhere I go, people remind me of him and tell me anecdotes."
[[nid:1471768]]
Millions of Peruvians hold darker memories of her father and refuse to vote for anyone bearing the Fujimori name, blocking her path to the presidency three times.
"In the last 25 years, we have been governed by anti-Fujimori governments," she said, making a single exception for Alan Garcia.
"All the others focused on insults and generating hatred and division among Peruvians."
Critics blame her and her party for much of Peru's political instability, citing Fuerza Popular's heavy influence and deal-making in Congress.
This was her first presidential campaign without her father, who died in 2024.
With crime now the top concern for voters, she leaned into his legacy under a single word: "order."
"I believe Peruvians want a Fujimori," she said. "Here I am."
Those close to Fujimori describe her as persistent, disciplined and relentless.
"Every blow life has dealt her has not broken her, it has made her stronger than anyone could imagine," her vice-presidential running mate Miki Torres told AFP.
She spent more than a year in pretrial detention while under investigation for alleged money laundering linked to the Odebrecht corruption scandal.
The case remains unresolved.
Long seen as confrontational, Fujimori has tried to soften her image and present herself as more conciliatory.
"I have made mistakes," she said during a presidential debate. "I learned from them and came back much stronger."
Keiko, whose name in Japanese means "blessed child," is widely known as "La China" – a school nickname referencing her Asian features.
The divorced mother of two daughters, aged 18 and 16, once said that "learning to be a mother is harder than running for president."
"The bar is high, and I hope to clear it," she said.
* The writers are from AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Civil servants drive Malaysia's rise in global competitiveness, says Anwar
Article:
ALOR GAJAH: Malaysia's improved performance in the 2026 World Competitiveness Ranking by the International Institute for Management Development (IMD) is largely due to the efficiency and commitment of the country's civil service, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today.
Anwar praised civil servants, describing them as the backbone of the achievement, which has also drawn recognition from several world leaders, including Turkmenistan President Serdar Berdimuhamedov during his recent visit to the country.
"Our achievements over the past three and a half years are not because of Anwar as prime minister, but because of the system (administration and the efficiency of the civil service) and the colleagues within the government team.
"The President (Serdar) mentioned that our performance in the World Competitiveness Index was very impressive. I thanked him, but I know this happened because of the public service team, because that efficiency and service are not the result of my efforts alone, but those of the entire team throughout the country," he said.
He was speaking during a meet-and-greet session with civil servants from the Southern Zone at the Public Works Department's Centre of Excellence for Engineering and Technology (Create) here today.
Also present were Melaka Chief Minister Datuk Seri Ab Rauf Yusoh, Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar, and Public Service Department director-general Tan Sri Wan Ahmad Dahlan Abd Aziz.
Anwar said that despite challenges in the government's efforts to eradicate corruption and the culture of commissions, the efficiency and commitment of civil servants had contributed significantly to Malaysia's improved standing in the global ranking.
At the same time, he acknowledged that efforts to reform the country and eliminate corrupt practices faced major obstacles, including resistance from groups that had long benefited from the existing system.
He said opposition to change did not only come from ordinary people but also involved elites, influential figures and those in positions of authority who had become accustomed to unhealthy practices.
Anwar said the phenomenon of "resistance to change" often emerged when the government took action against corruption and sought to improve the performance of the public service.
"The culture of resisting change must be overcome if the country is to continue progressing and earn international respect.
"The success of administrative reforms requires political courage as well as public support to ensure the nation is developed based on integrity and good governance," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Immigration raid uncovers filthy, overcrowded workers' hostel in Port Klang
Article:
PORT KLANG: A filthy and poorly maintained hostel believed to be housing foreign workers in cramped conditions was uncovered during a large-scale immigration enforcement operation at an industrial premises here today.
The two-storey building, located within a factory compound, was found to be dirty, foul-smelling and overcrowded when officers from the Immigration Department raided the premises under Ops Mega.
Members of the media accompanying the operation observed dozens of foreign workers living in congested conditions inside the structure, which is believed to have been built without approval.
The building comprised two floors, with five rooms on each level. Each room was believed to accommodate up to 10 occupants.
The living conditions were further worsened by open-concept toilet and bathing facilities, while the surrounding environment was found to be unhygienic and emitted an unpleasant odour.
Authorities also discovered a grocery shop operated by foreign nationals within the factory compound to cater to the daily needs of the hostel occupants.
The raid was conducted following two to three weeks of intelligence gathering by the Selangor Immigration Department on the presence and accommodation arrangements of foreign workers at the premises.
The operation, which began at 8am, involved 50 officers and personnel from the Immigration Department, Labour Department, Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry, and the Employees Provident Fund (EPF).
Selangor Immigration Department deputy director (Control) Mohamad Khusairi Kamarudin said enforcement teams raided two locations — a plastic manufacturing factory and a furniture factory — involving a total deployment of 100 officers and personnel.
At the first location, 66 foreign nationals from Bangladesh, Nepal and Myanmar were screened.
"Inspections resulted in the detention of 18 people comprising 16 Bangladeshis, one Nepali man and one Myanmar national, all men aged between 18 and 50," he said after the operation.
At the second location, authorities screened 520 foreign workers and detained 252 people, comprising 175 Bangladeshis, 33 Nepali men, four Myanmar nationals, 39 Pakistanis and one Sri Lankan.
Overall, 586 foreign nationals were screened, with 270 detained for various immigration-related offences.
Khusairi said preliminary checks revealed that most offences involved overstaying and misuse of immigration passes.
"Among the common offences detected were overstaying and misuse of passes.
"At the first location, several people claimed to possess valid documents but failed to produce them during inspections.
"Employers must remain vigilant regarding the status and whereabouts of their foreign workers. They must also ensure that Temporary Employment Visit Passes are used only at approved locations and within authorised employment sectors," he said.
He also advised foreign workers to carry identification and travel documents at all times to facilitate inspections by enforcement authorities.
Khusairi said similar operations would continue based on intelligence and public complaints.
"If complaints involve factories, we will conduct operations at factories.
"For now, action is being taken under the Immigration Act, while our investigation division will determine any further offences after detailed investigations are completed," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] No coalition govt: Onn Hafiz says BN will govern alone if it wins Johor
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Barisan Nasional (BN) Johor has ruled out forming a coalition government with any other political party after the Johor election, insisting its position is final and non-negotiable.
Johor BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said the coalition's stance would remain unchanged regardless of the election outcome, reiterating that BN intends to govern on its own if given the mandate.
"BN Johor will not form a coalition government with any other political party in this state," he said during the BN candidate announcement ceremony at the Johor Umno Liaison Office here today.
Onn Hafiz, who is also the caretaker Johor menteri besar, said BN would continue implementing its development agenda under the Maju Johor 2030 framework, describing it as a long-term commitment to the state.
He reminded candidates that their nomination was not a privilege but a responsibility entrusted to them by the party and the people.
He also urged all BN candidates to campaign with discipline, decorum and wisdom, in line with what he described as the "Johor way" of politics.
For those not selected, Onn Hafiz called on them to remain loyal and continue supporting the party's struggle.
"Our struggle is bigger than any individual or position. The one contesting may be a candidate, but the one that must win is all of us, the entire Barisan Nasional team," he said.
He added that BN was offering voters a stable, principled and structured government capable of sustaining Johor's development trajectory.
Onn Hafiz also urged voters to assess BN candidates based on their performance, track record and future plans.
Addressing criticism and political attacks, he said BN remained a coalition representing Malays, Chinese, Indians and other communities.
He stressed that BN's refusal to cooperate with certain parties was based on differences in ideology rather than race or trust.
Onn Hafiz also thanked party members for their cooperation in finalising the candidate list, noting that the selections had been reviewed and endorsed by BN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi.
The Johor election will be held on July 11, with nomination day on June 27 and early voting on July 7.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Starmer ally Jones backs Burnham after being reassured on economic plans
Article:
LONDON: Darren Jones, an ally of outgoing Prime Minister Keir Starmer, endorsed Andy Burnham to take over on Wednesday after saying he had been reassured by the former mayor's economic plans, including ways he could possibly borrow more.
Jones, chief secretary to Starmer, said he had discussed economic policy with Burnham after he alarmed investors last year by saying the country had to stop being in "hock to the bond markets", comments he later said had been ​misrepresented.
Asked by Sky News if Burnham would borrow more, Jones said markets, trade unions and the governing Labour Party would accept targeted additional borrowing if backed by a credible plan to stimulate the economy.
Jones said there was "room to borrow a little bit more" but added that Burnham had not outlined specific spending plans in their talks.
"We talked ... a lot about what he (Burnham) was thinking on the economy and what my thoughts were on that, and some of the potential ways forward for the new government," he said.
Starmer said on Monday he would step down following months of pressure over the party's poor poll ratings and setbacks in local elections, triggering a leadership contest to start on July 9.
Burnham is the only candidate to put his name forward and the party expects a coronation, meaning he could become prime minister by the middle of July.
If appointed, he will be Britain's seventh leader in 10 years.
"Andy Burnham is going to be the next prime minister. And if there was a contest of Labour Party members, he would win," Jones said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ministry hosts health screening for Media Prima staff in conjunction with Hawana
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Higher Education Ministry is strengthening cooperation with media practitioners through the Sihat Selalu #RakanMediaKPT programme, which offers comprehensive health screening services.
The programme, held at Balai Berita Media Prima Bhd (Media Prima) here today in conjunction with National Journalists' Day (Hawana) 2026, involves medical specialists from the University Malaya Medical Centre (UMMC).
Higher Education Ministry deputy secretary-general (policy) Ahmad Rizal Adnan said the initiative aims to promote a healthy lifestyle among media practitioners, who are often exposed to heavy workloads that may lead them to neglect self-care.
"The programme provides media practitioners with the opportunity to undergo basic health checks such as diabetes, blood pressure and cholesterol screening, as well as specialised screenings for colon, breast and cervical cancer.
"Early screening is crucial to detect diseases at an early stage, as we do not want them to be diagnosed only when they have reached a critical stage such as Stage 3, where treatment costs are significantly higher and recovery is more difficult," he said when met at the programme.
Meanwhile, Ahmad Rizal said that beyond health initiatives, the ministry through its Corporate Communications Unit is also planning to provide media practitioners with greater access to higher education via the Accreditation of Prior Experiential Learning (APEL) framework.
The initiative allows years of experience in the media industry to be converted into academic credits or university entry qualifications.
The ministry is currently planning to collaborate with various broadcasting agencies to introduce this special pathway, ensuring that the welfare and development of media practitioners continue to be safeguarded.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: E-hailing driver fined RM101,750 for importing zam-zam water
Article:
KLANG: An e-hailing driver was fined RM101,750 by the Magistrate's Court here today after being found guilty of importing prohibited goods, namely 5,500 bottles of zam-zam water, through West Port, Klang, five years ago.
Magistrate Mohd Hirman Ab Raub imposed the sentence on Syed Fazillah Syed Assimie, 41, after ruling that the prosecution had proven its case beyond reasonable doubt.
The court ordered him to pay a fine equivalent to 10 times the value of the goods, with one year's jail term in default.
Syed Fazillah was also ordered to pay customs duty amounting to 20 per cent of the value of the goods, totalling RM2,035, to the government through the Customs Department under Section 119A of the Customs Act 1967.
In addition, the court ordered all 5,500 bottles of zam-zam water to be forfeited to Customs under Section 127 of the same Act.
According to the charge, the father of three was accused of jointly importing 5,500 bottles of zam-zam water, each with a capacity of five litres, totalling 27,500 litres valued at RM10,175.
The offence was allegedly committed at about noon on June 2, 2021, at the CFS2 Warehouse, West Port, Klang.
He was charged under Section 135(1)(a) of the Customs Act 1967, read together with Section 34 of the Penal Code, and punishable under Section 135(1)(i)(aa) of the same Act.
During mitigation, defence counsel Gabriel Susayan urged the court to impose a reasonable fine, citing his client's irregular income as an e-hailing driver and family responsibilities.
However, prosecuting officer Shahrul Amin Shahrul Nizam sought a deterrent sentence, arguing that the illegal importation of zam-zam water could result in loss of tax revenue and undermine import control regulations.
The court allowed a stay of execution of the fine pending an appeal to the High Court and set bail at RM20,000.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Port Klang raid: Foreign worker found hiding in rubbish container, 252 detained
Article:
KLANG: A foreign worker was found hiding inside a rubbish container at a Port Klang furniture factory during a raid on illegal immigrants today.
The man, believed to be in his 30s, was discovered crouching inside a roll-on roll-off (RoRo) waste container during inspections under Ops Mega.
When questioned, he said he was resting during a break and denied trying to hide.
"I just wanted to rest because it's break time now," he told enforcement officers.
In the same operation, another foreign worker was found hiding beside a wood-cutting machine after allegedly panicking on seeing enforcement officers.
He said he was frightened and surprised by the presence of authorities while working at the factory.
The operation, led by the Selangor Immigration Department with other enforcement agencies, uncovered various attempts by undocumented migrants to evade arrest.
Selangor Immigration deputy director (Control) Mohamad Khusairi Kamarudin said 252 illegal immigrants were detained in the operation, which began at 8am.
He said 520 foreign nationals were inspected, with 252 men aged between 18 and 50 detained for various immigration offences.
"Those arrested comprised 175 Bangladeshis, 39 Pakistanis, 33 Nepalese, four Myanmar nationals and one Sri Lankan," he said at a press conference after the operation.
Mohamad Khusairi said offences included misuse of passes, failure to hold valid identification documents, overstaying and possession of unrecognised cards.
All detainees were taken to the Semenyih Immigration Depot for remand and further investigation under the Immigration Act 1959/63.
He said the department would not compromise with employers or individuals found to be harbouring or employing undocumented migrants.
"We will take firm action against anyone found protecting or employing illegal immigrants in violation of immigration laws," he said.
Mohamad Khusairi also urged the public to provide information on undocumented migrants or related activities.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Singapore, Indonesia, Malaysia and Brunei face high risk of severe haze this year
Article:
SINGAPORE: Singapore, Indonesia, Malaysia and Brunei face a high risk of severe haze this year due to hot and dry weather conditions, biofuel demand and economic pressures, a research institute said Wednesday.
The Singapore Institute of International Affairs said it was the second time it had issued a red risk rating since launching its Haze Outlook report in 2019.
The previous red risk rating was in 2023.
August to September is the peak danger period for haze in the Southeast Asian region, driven by the El Nino and Indian Ocean Dipole weather phenomena, the report said.
The return of El Nino is expected to create a longer and stronger dry season at a time when fire preparedness could be adversely affected by economic uncertainty and cost pressures.
The SIIA said rising costs of fertiliser and fuel as a result of the Iran war could lead to unsustainable activity such as the use of fire rather than machinery to clear land and dispose of waste.
Land use could also intensify as demand for biofuels rises due to energy supply disruptions.
"This trend will continue even if the US-Iran agreement holds, as countries now want energy independence," said SIIA associate director Khor Yu-Leng.
Asean cooperation and sustainable land management will be critical to reducing risks, the report said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Dewan Rakyat passes Road Transport (Amendment) Act 2026
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Dewan Rakyat today passed the Road Transport (Amendment) Act 2026, paving the way for stronger regulatory and enforcement powers under the Road Transport Act 1987.
The Bill was passed by a voice vote after being tabled for its second and third readings by Transport Minister Anthony Loke.
Loke said the amendments were necessary in light of Malaysia's persistently high road fatality rate.
"The need for these amendments is clear. In 2025, Malaysia recorded 6,537 deaths from road accidents.
"Of that number, 4,340 fatalities, or 66.4 per cent, involved motorcyclists and their passengers.
"These statistics show that road safety remains a major national challenge that requires more effective legislation, enforcement and education," he said in his winding-up speech after the second reading of the Bill today.
Among the key amendments is a proposal to
raise the maximum compound for traffic offences
from RM300 to RM500, effective Jan 1, 2029.
The revised compound rate will apply to offences including the improper display of vehicle registration numbers, operating a vehicle without a valid motor vehicle licence, and driving without a valid driving licence.
Other offences covered under the revised rate include speeding, failure to undergo mandatory periodic inspections for certain vehicles, failure to comply with traffic signals or enforcement instructions, and other basic road compliance offences.
The Bill also introduces a new Section 42A, making street racing and speed testing specific offences under the Act and enabling enforcement action to be taken before accidents, injuries or fatalities occur.
Under the provision, first-time offenders may be fined between RM2,000 and RM10,000, jailed for up to two years, or both.
For a second or subsequent offence, offenders may be fined between RM5,000 and RM20,000, jailed for up to five years, or both.
The amendments also seek to strengthen the digitalisation of core Road Transport Department services through the use of electronic documents and notices.
In addition, they introduce provisions to address illegal racing and regulate electric vehicles, special-purpose vehicles, modified vehicles, foreign-registered vehicles and cross-border vehicles.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bersatu only objects to PCM's entry into PN, not Pejuang
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bersatu has dismissed reports suggesting that the party had objected to Parti Pejuang Tanah Air's (Pejuang) entry into Perikatan Nasional (PN).
Bersatu information chief Datuk Tun Faisal Ismail Aziz, in dismissing the report published by a Malay daily, said the party only objected to Parti Cinta Malaysia's (PCM) entry into the opposition bloc.
He said the report – which claimed eight PN supreme council members opposed the inclusion of Parti Wawasan Negara (Wawasan) and Pejuang – was inaccurate, untrue and misleading.
"Bersatu supports Pejuang's entry into PN and only objects to PCM's inclusion.
"In view of this, Bersatu hopes the newspaper will immediately rectify the headline and content of its published report," he said in a Facebook post.
Tun Faisal said that, as Bersatu vice-president Datuk Seri Ahmad Faizal Azumu said, the objection against PCM was made because its entry could potentially trigger internal conflict within PN, alongside allegations that its application process was irregular.
"The application was submitted under the name of PCM, but the leadership information provided was under Wawasan, despite the party's name change and leadership restructuring having yet to be approved by the Registry of Societies (RoS)."
He added that in the same statement, Ahmad Faizal expressed regret over the actions of the PN chairman, who allegedly pressured the PN supreme council to hastily accept PCM's entry without considering the implications of the Societies Act 1966.
He said the move also allegedly failed to take into account the necessity of complying with existing laws.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: No adverse effects on Iran despite 'pragmatists' vs 'hardliners' rivalry
Article:
FACTIONAL divisions and opposition to talks with the United States exist within the Iranian hierarchy, but such splits will not suffice on their own to derail the negotiating process, analysts say.
After five weeks of war paused by an April ceasefire and ended by an accord this month, US Vice President JD Vance and Iran's top negotiator Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf held talks in Switzerland mediated by Qatar and Pakistan to begin a process in search of a final agreement.
Yet the path remains tortuous, with Iran aware of the leverage it can exert over the global economy through control of the Strait of Hormuz and President Donald Trump threatening new military action if talks fail.
And while Trump faces criticism of the deal from some conservatives at home, there have also been rumblings of discontent in Teheran over the talks with the US, a foe since the 1979 Islamic revolution known as the "Great Satan."
"There are certainly factions seriously opposed to the talks and to any compromises with the US," said Yale University lecturer Arash Azizi.
"But it is my assessment that they currently lack the institutional power necessary to block the talks or even do much to shape their outcome," he told AFP.
In a small, but unusual demonstration, dozens of people on June 13 protested against the talks outside Iranian foreign ministry offices in the northeastern city of Mashhad, shouting slogans against Ghalibaf and fellow negotiator Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi.
Iran's supreme leader Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei – still unseen in public since his father and predecessor, Ali Khamenei, was killed in an Israeli air strike on the first day of the war – said he had approved the deal and awaited "face-to-face" talks despite having a "different view."
Ghalibaf noted on X that a state television presenter said he wished Teheran airport had been closed so the negotiators could not leave for Switzerland.
"More blood would have been shed" in Lebanon, where Israel has been fighting Teheran-backed Hizbollah, if the team had not gone, Ghalibaf countered.
[[nid:1471704]]
There have also been reports of dissent from a handful of officials, most prominently the ultra-conservative former top nuclear negotiator and national security council chief Saeed Jalili.
"I don't believe that hardliners currently have the upper hand in Iran," said Sanam Vakil, director of the Middle East and North Africa Programme at Chatham House.
"I think that there is a favourable consensus to give negotiations a try and test out President Trump's appetite for a deal," she said.
In a sign talks have been blessed even among hardline elements within the security forces, Esmail Qaani, the head of the Revolutionary Guards' Quds Force, which is responsible for the ideological army's foreign operations, said Araghchi and Ghalibaf should be "praised."
In a rare appearance on state television, he said the "brothers behind the missile launchers" and the "brothers sitting at the negotiating table" were united by their "resistance."
During the G7 summit, Trump repeatedly, and somewhat unexpectedly, praised the current Iranian leadership, describing them as "smart", "very rational" and "not radicalised."
Vance told Sirius XM the president "was right" to say "we are dealing with more reasonable people" and Washington now believed "hardliners" had seen they had been making a "mistake" in their dealings with the US.
"The pragmatists within the Iranian system, the people who really do want to transform their relationship with the Middle East and with the world, those people are winning the argument," Vance added at a White House briefing.
Araghchi and Ghalibaf have been at pains to make clear they won't yield on Iranian interests, with the foreign minister comparing the uncompromising stance to the heroics of national football team goalkeeper Alireza Beiranvand in the World Cup.
They have also avoided having any photograph taken with Vance that would be hailed in some quarters as historic but could give fuel to enemies.
The International Crisis Group described the Iranian leadership system as "historically opaque" and now "profoundly disrupted by the killing of many of its leaders early in the war, as well as the constant fear of further strikes."
Azizi also said that while anti-Americanism was still fundamental to the system, "the new leaders are less ideological than (Ali) Khamenei and might even go on to transform the regime."
* The writer is from AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kedah Sultan visits Sultan Abdul Samad Building
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Sultan of Kedah, Al Aminul Karim Sultan Sallehuddin Sultan Badlishah, today visited the Sultan Abdul Samad Building (BSAS), a heritage landmark that played a significant role in Malaysia's history.
His Royal Highness, who arrived at about 10.30am, was received by Khazanah Nasional managing director Datuk Amirul Feisal Wan Zahir.
Also present were Khazanah Nasional chief corporate officer and general legal adviser Datuk Mohamed Nasri Sallehuddin and head of real assets Selvendran Katheerayson.
During the visit, the Sultan toured the Confluence Hall, which houses a gallery on the origins and development of Kuala Lumpur, and was briefed by Think City senior manager Mariana Isa on its exhibits.
His Royal Highness also visited the Visionary Hall, featuring models and multimedia displays related to the capital city's development, before proceeding to the balcony above the Porte Cochere.
The Sultan later visited the School of Hard Knocks, operated by Royal Selangor, and attended a light luncheon before departing BSAS at about 1.15pm.
Amirul Feisal said the royal visit was deeply appreciated and was significant to Khazanah Nasional's efforts to preserve the heritage building, which extend beyond conserving its physical structure to highlighting the history of Kuala Lumpur, Selangor and Malaysia's development.
He said BSAS has received about 200,000 visitors since opening to the public on Feb 2.
"The visit by His Royal Highness the Sultan of Kedah is a tremendous honour and recognition of our efforts to preserve the nation's history, which is meaningful not only to society but also to the royal institution," he said.
On Jan 31, His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, officiated the completion of Phase One restoration works at BSAS following 11 months of conservation under the Khazanah Heritage Fund programme.
Originally known as the Secretariat Building, BSAS once served as the centre of the nation's administration and witnessed the lowering of the Union Jack and the raising of the Federation of Malaya flag for the first time in 1957.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor Umno Youth reminds leaders to remain loyal ahead of state election
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Johor Umno Youth today reminded party leaders to remain loyal to Umno and BN following concerns raised by Umno Supreme Council member Datuk Dr Mohd Puad Zarkashi over the coalition's candidates for the upcoming state election.
Its chief Noor Azleen Ambros said loyalty to the party should not be dependent on positions or personal interests, stressing that members must respect decisions made by the party leadership.
"The culture of threatening or provoking the party when we are dissatisfied is not the Johor way, nor is it Umno's culture," he said when met at the Johor Umno liaison office here today.
Earlier, Puad hinted at a major announcement on Saturday, claiming that "something is not right" within Johor Umno after reviewing BN's candidate list.
Sources said the former Rengit assemblyman was unhappy with BN's choice of candidate for the seat, which differed from his recommendation.
When contacted, Puad declined to
elaborate
, saying only that "Johor Umno is not independent".
Noor Azleen said Puad, as a senior party leader, should set a positive example for younger members by demonstrating his commitment to the party.
"He should show the younger generation what loyalty means. This party belongs to all of us, not to any individual," he said.
He added that BN's strength has always been rooted in collective leadership and discipline, particularly during challenging times.
"BN is bigger than any individual. The party's leaders have vast experience and understand what is best for the coalition and its future.
"Candidate selection is not a decision made lightly. It involves thorough consideration at various levels of the party and coalition leadership," he said.
Noor Azleen said members should focus on ensuring BN's victory in the state election rather than creating unnecessary distractions that could weaken party unity.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Trump orders probe as gas prices fail to fall fast enough
Article:
WASHINGTON: Donald Trump said on Wednesday he ordered an investigation into major oil companies over high gasoline prices as the US leader faces criticism over the impact of the Mideast war.
Global petroleum prices soared after Iran blocked the flow of oil through the vital Strait of Hormuz in response to US-Israeli strikes in February.
"The big Oil Companies are not dropping their price at the pump commensurate with the sharply lower prices they are paying for Oil," Trump wrote on Truth Social.
"Those prices are dropping like a rock! In other words, customers are being 'gouged'," he added.
Gasoline prices are a political issue in the United States, where fossil fuel-powered vehicles are many Americans primary means of transport.
Trump has faced criticism for launching the war and its impact on the fuel costs paid be millions of Americans as November's congressional elections draw closer.
The president repeatedly predicted that fuel prices would "come down like a rock" after the conflict ended.
But economists dispute that claim, expecting oil prices to take months to return to pre-war levels.
Tehrran and Washington have struck an initial deal that includes the resumption of oil tanker traffic through Hormuz, though issues like Iran's nuclear programme are still in dispute.
Gasoline prices are still above their pre-war levels, but have come down since the initial deal was announced.
The average price of a gallon (3.8 litres) of regular gasoline logged in at US$3.93 on Tuesday, according to the AAA motor club.
Trump has faced backlash from Americans who accuse him of pouring billions of taxpayer dollars into the Middle East war while oil prices and inflation skyrocket in the United States.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man to face 207 charges for signing contracts as a 'director' while bankrupt
Article:
NIBONG TEBAL: A man is expected to be charged tomorrow after he allegedly acted as a company director and entered into multiple contracts while an undischarged bankrupt.
The suspect is alleged to have signed six Letters of Award and issued 201 official company letters under the designation in the course of business dealings.
Police said he will face 207 charges under Section 109(1)(m)(ii) of the Insolvency Act.
Upon conviction, each charge carries a fine, a jail term of up to two years, or both.
He is scheduled to appear before the Jawi Magistrate's Court at 9am.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Court orders mental evaluation for man who pleaded guilty to royal insults online
Article:
SEPANG: The Sessions Court today ordered a 37-year-old unemployed man to undergo psychiatric evaluation after he pleaded guilty to insulting and threatening members of the Terengganu royal family online.
Judge Ahmad Fuad Othman directed Azman Hakim Hassanuddin to be assessed at Hospital Bahagia Ulu Kinta in Perak.
This comes following an application by deputy public prosecutor Fadhli Ab Wahab under Section 342 of the Criminal Procedure Code, which deals with procedure where an accused is suspected to be of unsound mind.
The court fixed Aug 26 for mention to update the court on the assessment report before sentencing.
Earlier, Azman Hakim admitted to uploading grossly offensive and menacing images as well as postings against the royal family.
The offences occurred between 2.25pm and 4.29pm on Feb 19, which was viewed the next day at 6.30pm at Menara MCMC in Cyberjaya.
The offence was framed under Section 233(1)(a) of the Communication and Multimedia Act punishable under Section 233(2) of the same act.
It carries a maximum RM500,000 fine or a jail term not exceeding two years or both upon conviction.
Separately, Fuad fined two online users — 55-year-old Chong Kwai Fee and 45-year-old Romy Irwan Shah Amri Irawan — RM4,000 each, in default six months jail, for uploading false posts related to Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, the oil crisis and an image on the new electricity tariff.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: BN announces Johor election candidate lists, Onn Hafiz spearheading the charge
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, June 24 — Barisan Nasional (BN) announced its list of 56 candidates for the Johor election today, ending speculation over which candidates the coalition would field.
The list features a mix of new faces and seasoned leaders from BN’s component parties, namely Umno, MCA and MIC.
Johor BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi announced the list of candidates for the coalition that will contest in the upcoming Johor election.
Onn, who leads the list as the Machap state seat candidate and incumbent, reminded those present that being an election candidate is not a privilege.
On the contrary, he said it is a great responsibility and trust given to the candidates to carry out their duties as best as possible.
“I would like to remind candidates to campaign with mindful conduct and wisdom because in Johor we have our own ways.
“To those who were not selected as candidates, I appeal for them to continue the struggle together.
“Our struggle is bigger than any individual or position. The one contesting may be one candidate, but the one who must win is all of us, and the entire Barisan team,” he said at the BN Johor state electron candidate announcement ceremony at the Johor Umno state liaison office here today.
Present were respective component party and affiliate group leaders such as MCA president Datuk Seri Wee Ka Siong; MIC president Tan Sri S.A. Vigneswaran; People’s Progressive Party (PPP) president Datuk Dr J. Loga Bala Mohan and Umno Youth chief Datuk Dr Muhamad Akmal Saleh.
Onn Hafiz, who is also the Johor caretaker menteri besar, also called on all candidates to offer the people a stable government.
He added that Johoreans need a government with clear principles, vision, structured administration and the ability to continue the progress of Maju Johor 2030 agenda.
“We have never promised the moon and stars. We also do not promise that this journey will be an easy one,” he said, adding that Johor BN will continue working hard to develop the state.
Onn, who is also the apparent menteri besar designate, called on Johoreans to judge BN based on its work, achievements and plans for the future.
“Therefore, let us all close ranks and move on one team,” he said.
